title: Guardian (Executor-class)
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg|italics=1|title2=''Executor''-class}} {{Youmay|the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]]|[[Guardian (disambiguation)|another use of the word]]}} {{Update|[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]}} {{IndividualShip |image=[[File:Guardian.png]] |name=''Guardian'' |designer= |manufacturer=[[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]][[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] |line= |model= |type=[[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]]While [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] describes the vessel as an 8,000 meter [[Super-class Star Destroyer|''Super''-class Star Destroyer]], the class has since been [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]], in sources such as the [[Databank (original)|Databank's]] [http://www.starwars.com/databank/starship/superstardestroyer/index.html Super Star Destroyer] entry, to the 19,000 meter ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought. |cost= |modifier= |sysmods= |length=19,000 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width= |height= |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed= |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive=*Class 2.0 *Class 10 {{C|[[Backup hyperdrive|backup]]}} Note: Both hyperdrives inoperative from [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] to c. Calamari]]''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Gaen Drommel]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] *[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] |navy=*[[Oplovis Sector Fleet]]{{CSWECite|II|22|Harrod}} *[[New Republic Third Fleet]] *[[Fleet Group One]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' *[[First Fleet (Galactic Alliance)|Galactic Alliance First Fleet]] |fleet= |owners=Gaen Drommel |captains=*[[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Gaen Drommel *Colonel Niovi (acting) *Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] |crewmembers=*[[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Eijul]] *Colonel [[Gastos Niovi|Niovi]] |registry= |aliases= }} {{Quote|Well, technically, this ship isn't a Star Destroyer.''"
"''Thank the Force for small favors.''"
"''It's more of a Super Star Destroyer.|Cryle Cavv and Sienn Sconn|}} The '''''Guardian''''' was an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]]. Originally under the command of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gaen Drommel]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], it was stationed near [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] when news of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] arrived. Drommel took the ''Guardian'' to the region of space surrounding his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Oplovis]], where he became a [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]]. After a series of attacks on [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] worlds and bases, the tide turned for Drommel, and the ''Guardian'' was badly damaged at the [[Battle of Tantive V]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Drommel fled into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], but due to the damage sustained on the vessel's [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], the ''Guardian'' emerged in the [[Fardon system]] rather than its intended destination. Its hyperdrive inoperable, it went into orbit around the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Soullex]], where it would stay for over a decade as Drommel conducted repairs. Around [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], the New Republic learned that the ''Guardian'' was receiving parts through its [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttles]]. Two ship thieves, [[Cryle Cavv]] and [[Sienn Sconn]], were sent to track the shuttles back to their origin and capture the ''Guardian''. After freeing a number of New Republic prisoners, Cavv and Sconn were able to reprogram the vessel's nearly-repaired hyperdrive to leap into the middle of a New Republic fleet when Drommel tested it. Drommel refused to surrender, but his officer [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Gastos Niovi|Niovi]] shot him and took command of the vessel, surrendering it to the New Republic. The New Republic assigned the ''Guardian'' to the [[New Republic Third Fleet|Third Fleet]], under the command of Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]]. From the ''Guardian'', Ackbar led the New Republic's fleets in a series of skirmishes against the Empire, including the [[Second Battle of Champala]] and the [[Battle of Anx Minor]], which drove the Empire under [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] back to a scant few sectors. In the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the ''Guardian'' served as protection for [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Cal Omas]], before being deployed to [[Traest Kre'fey]]'s [[Fleet Group One]] at [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. After the formation of a new government, the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], the ''Guardian'' was redeployed to [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] to protect the Alliance headquarters. It later fought against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] in the [[Defense of Mon Calamari (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Mon Calamari]]. ==Description== The ''Guardian'' was one of the 19-kilometer [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnoughts]]. Holding over 280,000 normally, this number declined in the decade it sat over [[Soullex]], down to 250,000—including the nearly 40,000 [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] aboard. The vessel was equipped with a number of [[IM-455 modular garrison|prefabricated garrison bases]], which its commander, [[Gaen Drommel]], used to subjugate the [[Fardon system]] after the ''Guardian''{{'s}} escape from [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces at the [[Battle of Tantive V]]. It was normally armed with 250 each of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]], [[Heavy turbolaser turret/Legends|heavy turbolaser]], and [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missile]] emplacements, as well as 40 [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] installations; however, after [[Tantive V]], these numbers were greatly reduced, in some cases over half of the emplacements having been taken out of commission. The [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]], too, normally classing 2.0 with a class 10 backup, were rendered inoperative until their repair by Drommel's forces. The ship held a complement of a number of [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class T-4a shuttles]], which Drommel used in the ''Guardian''{{'s}} downtime as the main mode of transportation to gather materials for the vessel's repair. The ''Guardian'' possessed a cavernous command deck, near which the [[Admiral/Legends|admiral's]] quarters were located. It had an enormous admiral's lounge, which under New Republic ownership was elegantly furnished and decorated with flowers. The vessel had a detention block as well, to hold its prisoners; under Drommel's command, many of these were New Republic personnel who had inadvertently found the ''Guardian''. Interrogation rooms were available for the questioning of these individuals. The ship's hyperdrive was accessible from the engineering room; coordinates could be input directly through there, bypassing the [[Navigation computer/Legends|navigation computer]]. ==History== ===Under Drommel's command=== Following its completion, the ''Guardian'' was placed under the command of Admiral Gaen Drommel of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] as a reward for Drommel's [[Battle of Aargonar (Galactic Civil War)|destruction]] of [[Aargonar 3 rebel base|Rebel bases]] on [[Aargonar 3]], [[Randa/Legends|Randa]], and the [[Valsedian asteroid belt]].{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-3|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 3|nolive=1}} At the time of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the ''Guardian'' was stationed near [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The battle saw the death of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the defeat of the Imperial fleet at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]; when Drommel learned of this, he fled Coruscant in the ''Guardian'' for the region of space around his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Oplovis]], where he intended to use his fleet to set up a personal empire of his own. Under Drommel's command, the ''Guardian'' and three [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] set out shortly after Endor on a campaign of terror against the safeworlds and bases of the New Republic, with devastating effect on the fledgling government. [[File:Guardian-SWAJ15p228.png|thumb|left|190px|The damaged ''Guardian'']] Drommel's campaign soon came to an end, though. The New Republic had been studying the weaknesses of Imperial Star Destroyers, and when they engaged Drommel's fleet at the Battle of Tantive V in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' they were able to use this to their greatest advantage. Two of Drommel's [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] were destroyed, the third was taken, and with the Republic targeting the weapon and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] systems of the ''Guardian'' and crippling the hyperdrive, the vessel was heavily damaged and forced to retreat. The [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] was able to make the leap to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], but it did not arrive at its intended destination, instead emerging into the Fardon system of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. With its hyperdrive inactive, the crippled vessel stationed itself above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Soullex, where it was barely able to maintain orbit. Drommel subjugated the system, deploying three prefabricated garrison bases to the world below. Drommel set to work repairing the ''Guardian'', dispatching ''Lambda''-class shuttles to Imperial-controlled worlds for assistance. Drommel planned to conduct his repairs as quickly as possible, but with the remnants of the Empire falling to the New Republic, Imperial worlds were unable to spare any aid for him. Drommel intended to use the ''Guardian'' as the centerpiece of a campaign to destroy the New Republic, and he therefore kept his search for parts and assistance as quiet as possible. Around [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Drommel began receiving the necessary parts to repair the vessel's hyperdrive, but the ship would remain where it was for over a decade. In the course of that time, a number of New Republic personnel unknowingly hyperjumped into the Fardon system, only to be detained by the ''Guardian''. [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] operative [[Shandria L'hnnar]] led an assault team to find and locate the vessel, but they too were captured. ===Capture=== {{Quote|The ''Guardian'' is still out there, somewhere, battered and broken. Just waiting for us.|Airen Cracken|}} New Republic [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] eventually began running into Drommel's ''Lambda'' shuttles as they retrieved parts. Further investigation revealed that the vessels were part of the ''Guardian''{{'s}} complement. In [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' [[Cryle Cavv]], a ship thief in the employ of the New Republic, learned from his contacts of a meeting taking place on the planet [[Vohai]] between [[black marketeer]] [[Burgo Teage]] and an Imperial ''Lambda'' shuttle, regarding the sale of hyperdrive parts. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] of New Republic Intelligence realized that taking the ''Guardian'' would be a major coup for the Republic, and he and Cavv recruited Cavv's nephew [[Sienn Sconn]], another ship thief. Cavv and Sconn were able to disrupt the meeting on Vohai, seize the shuttle, and follow its navigation computer back to its origin point at Soullex. While docking with the ''Guardian'', Cavv and Sconn disabled the shuttle's [[Comm unit/Legends|comm unit]] to prevent having to speak to the vessel's comm operators and giving the ruse away. Upon docking, they ambushed a pair of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and donned the soldiers' armor as a disguise. They were also able to capture a [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]], [[CT-EX]], and reprogram it to their purposes. Drommel, meanwhile, was alerted to the ship's communications problem and, suspicious, had the shuttle examined. When the search team found the [[Stun/Legends|stunned]] stormtroopers, Drommel put out an alert for the impostors. He ordered repairs on the hyperdrive sped up, intending to leave the system and return to Imperial space in the next 36 hours. [[File:Guardian_Leaps.png|thumb|right|220px|The ''Guardian'' makes its first leap after repairs.]] Cavv and Sconn, however, were already on their way to the Super Star Destroyer's Detention Block 220, where the New Republic prisoners were kept. All guards but one had been sent out to find the intruders, and the remaining guard was quickly disabled. The thieves freed the prisoners, including Shandria L'hnnar, then recruited them into their plan. Cavv and Sconn set off for the engineering room, intending to manually set the hyperdrive to jump into New Republic-friendly space, and signaling CT-EX as their fallback plan. They arrived as Drommel was receiving the go-ahead to jump within the next ten minutes. Their tampering, however, triggered an alarm, and Drommel returned with a phalanx of soldiers to take them into custody. His interrogation proved fruitless; at the same time, however, CT-EX was reprogramming the hyperdrive's coordinates. Drommel sent the thieves back to the detention cells in preparation for the test of the hyperdrive, but they were intercepted by L'hnnar and the other prisoners. Drommel ordered the ship to jump. The ''Guardian'' made the leap successfully, into the middle of a New Republic fleet led by the [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] ''[[Equity]]''. ''Equity''{{'s}} captain, [[Volahn]], ordered the ''Guardian'' to surrender. Drommel refused, and the entire New Republic infiltration party—Cavv, Sconn, and the prisoners—forced its way onto the command deck, drawing their weapons on the deck staff and being drawn on in turn. Cavv spoke to the crew, telling them that peacefully handing over the vessel would help their case with the New Republic. Drommel had had enough and ordered the crew to open fire, but Sconn persuaded them to back down and lower their weapons. In a rage, Drommel drew a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] on Cavv, but the admiral's subordinate, [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Gastos Niovi]], stopped Drommel, strangling him to death. As acting commander, Niovi surrendered the ''Guardian'' to the New Republic. ===In New Republic service=== With the ''Guardian'' in its possession, the New Republic set about refitting the vessel. A year after its capture, the New Republic added the ship to its [[New Republic Third Fleet|Third Fleet]]. Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] took command of the vessel, and under his leadership the Third and [[New Republic Fifth Fleet|Fifth Fleets]] engaged Imperial Admiral [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] in a number of major battles, most notable among them the [[Second Battle of Champala]] and the [[Battle of Anx Minor]]. At [[Anx Minor/Legends|Anx Minor]], Ackbar emerged victorious at the last minute after concentrating fire on the engines of the experimental [[Rakehell (Sorannan-class)|''EX-F'']], which exploded and took out the surrounding Imperial Star Destroyers. Under Ackbar, the ''Guardian'', and the fleets' onslaught, Pellaeon was forced to retreat and lost significant territory. The ''Guardian'' remained in New Republic custody into the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. In the weeks prior to the [[Battle of Ebaq]] 9 in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], which would prove to be a major victory against the invading [[Yuuzhan Vong]], New Republic [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Cal Omas]] went into isolation aboard the ''Guardian'', which remained safely cruising between the stars for the duration of the battle. Upon the Republic's victory, the ''Guardian'' joined Admiral [[Traest Kre'fey]]'s [[Fleet Group One]] at [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. There, Omas and [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] met in the vessel's admiral's lounge to discuss the formation of a new government, the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]]. When the Galactic Alliance established itself on the planet [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]], the ''Guardian'' was recalled to join the planet's home fleet. In [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]], word came of a massive Yuuzhan Vong [[Defense of Mon Calamari (Yuuzhan Vong War)|assault]] being planned on Mon Calamari. The Alliance used the opportunity to deploy half its fleets for the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|retaking]] of Coruscant, which had fallen two years before, while keeping the other half to defend Mon Calamari; ''Guardian'' was among the latter half, and took up station at [[Mon Calamari Extreme]], a position in the far reaches of the [[Mon Calamari system/Legends|Calamari system]]. When the Yuuzhan Vong vessels arrived and began sending out their waves of [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], the Alliance countered with its own starfighter attack to clear firing lanes between the enemy [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] and their own. As lanes opened up, the starfighters evacuated those areas, and the ''Guardian'' and companion vessel [[Harbinger (Mon Calamari Star Cruiser)|''Harbinger'']] moved forward to fire salvos into the heart of the Yuuzhan Vong fleet. As they moved further into the system, the Yuuzhan Vong diverted their [[dovin basal]] shielding towards the front of the fleet's advance. The Alliance sent Star Destroyers in to flank them, forcing them to relocate the shields and allow ''Guardian'', ''Harbinger'', and the [[Viscount-class Star Defender|Mon Calamari Star Defender]] [[Viscount (Viscount-class)|''Viscount'']] to continue their barrage from the front. Alliance bombers then moved against the Yuuzhan Vong fleet, and the enemy vessels shifted their dovin basals to protect themselves, freeing ''Guardian'' to wipe out waves of [[yorik-et]] starfighters instead. The Yuuzhan Vong soon pulled a flotilla away from their fleet to bolster against the concurrent Galactic Alliance attacks elsewhere, but even with the two opposing armadas on more equal parity, the Alliance continued to fall back toward Mon Calamari. On the verge of being forced to retreat, the Alliance was reprieved by the arrival of the planet [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]] in the [[Coruscant system/Legends|Coruscant system]], which convinced Yuuzhan Vong [[Warmaster]] [[Nas Choka]] to call off the attack on Mon Calamari and divert his resources to Coruscant. ==Commanders and crew== ===Admiral Gaen Drommel=== [[File:Gaen_Drommel_AJ15.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Gaen Drommel]] {{Main|Gaen Drommel}} Gaen Drommel was a charismatic commander of the ''Guardian'' and leader of his men. Feared and respected, he commanded the loyalty of his soldiers. Having learned the use of fear as a motivator from [[Wilhuff Tarkin/Legends|Wilhuff Tarkin]], Drommel was harsh with those who disobeyed his orders or displeased him with their work, executing them on the spot. He took a disliking to those who were not thorough and made assumptions, and the men under his command made sure to have all the information before telling him anything they assumed. Drommel's strict methods earned him great obedience from his soldiers; they would always double-check their work with him watching, and if he needed a task done quicker than expected, his men would work extra-hard and without breaks until it was complete. The admiral was alert to goings-on aboard his ship, personally supervising important repairs and aware of the deeds of intruders and saboteurs who could threaten his work. As the commander of his ship, Drommel was a would-be [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], intending to use the ''Guardian'' first to carve out his own territory from the remains of the Empire, and later as the central piece of an attempt to eliminate the New Republic. However, his refusal to surrender and willingness to fight to the end cost him his life, when he was killed by Colonel Niovi for not capitulating to the New Republic forces holding him and the ship's command deck crew at gunpoint. Upon Drommel's death, Niovi assumed acting command of the ''Guardian'', enough to surrender it to New Republic forces. ===Admiral Gial Ackbar=== {{Main|Gial Ackbar/Legends}} Under the command of Gial Ackbar, who had led the fleets of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and its successor the New Republic for most of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the ''Guardian'' saw great victory for the New Republic. A distinguished strategist, Ackbar oversaw the ''Guardian''{{'s}} campaign against Gilad Pellaeon of the [[Imperial Remnant]]. Along with the Third and Fifth Fleets, the ''Guardian'' under Ackbar fought at [[Champala/Legends|Champala]], defeated the experimental ''EX-F'' at Anx Minor, and drove Pellaeon into retreat and the Remnant back to eight sectors in the Outer Rim, ultimately leading the Empire to sue for peace. ==Behind the scenes== The ''Guardian'' initially appeared as a sidebar in [[Louis J. Prosperi]]'s [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game|roleplaying game]] sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], as part of Gaen Drommel's character profile. As the book was set in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], its information on the ''Guardian'' ends with the vessel in orbit over Soullex. The sourcebook provides a number of scenarios for the players to find the ''Guardian'', including tracking a ''Lambda''-class shuttle back to Soullex, replacing its crew and boarding the ''Guardian'', finding the Super Star Destroyer while [[Scout/Legends|scouting]] the Fardon system, and making an emergency leap from a battle that ends up at Soullex. ''[[Two for One]]'', a short story by [[Paul Danner]] from [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 15|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 15]], would later use several of these ideas in telling the story of how the New Republic captured the ''Guardian''. From then on the ''Guardian'' would make several appearances in novels and [[Star Wars: Essential Guides|Essential Guides]] under New Republic custody. In ''Wanted by Cracken'', ''Guardian'' is listed as an 8,000 meter [[Super-class Star Destroyer|''Super''-class Star Destroyer]]. Since then, that class has been [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]], in sources such as the [[Databank (original)|Databank's]] Super Star Destroyer entry{{DB|starship|superstardestroyer|Super Star Destroyer}} and [[Starship Battles (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Starship Battles'']], to be the 19,000 meter ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought. The [[:File:Guardian.png|original image]] of the ''Guardian'' in ''Wanted by Cracken'', however, shows it much closer to the 19 kilometer length—approximately eleven to twelve times longer than the Imperial Star Destroyers in the picture—than the 8 kilometer size listed in the statistics. The ''Guardian'' was definitively stated to be 19,000 meters in the [[Star Wars Blog]] article, ''[[The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire]]''. ==Appearances== *{{JournalCite|15|Two for One}} {{1st}} *''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]'' *''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]'' ==Sources== *[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] {{1stm}} *{{JournalCite|2|Recon & Report: The Journey to Coruscant|reprint=1}} *{{JournalCite|15|ISB Intercepts}} *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' *{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-3|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 3|nolive=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |da=Guardian |de=Guardian |es=Guardián (clase Ejecutor) }} [[Category:Captured starships]] [[Category:Executor-class Star Dreadnoughts]] [[Category:Flagships]] [[Category:Starships of the Galactic Alliance]] [[Category:Starships of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Starships of the Imperial splinter factions]] [[Category:Starships of the New Republic]]
